channel.name: Shadow Empires
channel.tagline: Unmasking the greed, secrets, and scandals that built the world's most powerful empires.
channel.description: From blood diamonds to toxic monopolies, we pull back the curtain on history's most ruthless business empires. New documentaries weekly.

long_form_duration.rule: Prioritize retention over padding. Scripts should be long enough to clear YouTube mid-roll eligibility, but scenes must earn their time with new facts, tension, or payoff.

shorts_duration.rule: Use Shorts for reach and funneling viewers to long-form videos; do not treat Shorts as the primary watch-hours path.

viral_ideas.title: How One Family Fooled the World into Buying Worthless Rocks
viral_ideas.topic: The De Beers diamond cartel, artificial scarcity, and marketing scams.
viral_ideas.hook: A diamond is forever. A marketing slogan so powerful it tricked the entire human race into buying common stones. This is the dark history of the De Beers empire.
viral_ideas.thumbnail_concept: A hand holding a glowing blue diamond in a dark mine, bold red text: 'THEY FOOLED YOU'.

viral_ideas.title: The Poisoned Formula: The Corporate Giant That Stole Africa's Markets
viral_ideas.topic: Nestlé's aggressive baby formula campaign in developing nations.
viral_ideas.hook: In the 1970s, one food conglomerate launched a sales campaign so aggressive it led to thousands of infant deaths. This is how they captured a continent.
viral_ideas.thumbnail_concept: A classic baby bottle filled with dark sludge against a bright corporate skyscraper, bold red text: 'TOXIC GREED'.

viral_ideas.title: The Secret Society That Controlled the World's Electricity
viral_ideas.topic: The Phoebus Cartel, planned obsolescence, and lightbulb conspiracies.
viral_ideas.hook: In 1924, a group of businessmen gathered in a smoke-filled room in Geneva. Their goal? Make lightbulbs burn out faster. And they got away with it.
viral_ideas.thumbnail_concept: A vintage glowing lightbulb with a dollar sign filament, bold red text: 'THE CARTEL'.

viral_ideas.title: The Billion-Dollar Poison: How America's Sweetest Brand Fooled a Generation
viral_ideas.topic: The sugar lobby's bribery of scientists in the 1960s to blame fat for heart disease.
viral_ideas.hook: For fifty years, you were told that fat makes you fat. It was a lie. A lie bought and paid for by three Harvard scientists and a bag of cash.
viral_ideas.thumbnail_concept: Sugar cubes stacked like a skull, bold text: 'THE LIE'.

viral_ideas.title: The Company That Literally Conquered India (and Went Bankrupt)
viral_ideas.topic: The rise and fall of the East India Company.
viral_ideas.hook: It had its own army of 260,000 men. It ruled over 150 million people. It was the first corporation too big to fail.
viral_ideas.thumbnail_concept: An old British ship sailing into a stormy sea of coins, bold text: 'THE EMPEROR'.

viral_ideas.title: The Toxic Empire: The Family That Hooked America on Pain
viral_ideas.topic: The Sackler family, Purdue Pharma, and the OxyContin epidemic.
viral_ideas.hook: They wore expensive suits, donated to world-class museums, and built a dynasty. Behind it all was a chemical that destroyed millions of families.
viral_ideas.thumbnail_concept: A golden pill bottle leaking glowing liquid, bold text: 'PUNISHED?'.

viral_ideas.title: The Secret Monopoly That Controls Everything You Wear
viral_ideas.topic: Luxottica's dominance of the eyewear industry.
viral_ideas.hook: Why do plastic glasses cost $400? Because whether you buy Oakley, Ray-Ban, or Prada, you are buying from the exact same company. Welcome to the illusion of choice.
viral_ideas.thumbnail_concept: A pair of sunglasses reflecting a single giant eyeball, bold text: 'NO CHOICE'.

viral_ideas.title: The Bananapocalypse: The Fruit Company That Overthrew Governments
viral_ideas.topic: United Fruit Company, CIA operations, and banana republics.
viral_ideas.hook: A single fruit company controlled the railways, the mail, and the politicians of Central America. And when one president stood in their way, they called in the CIA.
viral_ideas.thumbnail_concept: A banana peeled to reveal a military tank, bold text: 'FRUIT CO.'.
viral_ideas.ctr_angle: Bizarre historical facts and dark irony.

viral_ideas.title: The Tech Empire That Sold Out the Free World
viral_ideas.topic: Yahoo's complicity in Chinese dissident tracking.
viral_ideas.hook: Before Google was a giant, Yahoo ruled the web. But in a desperate attempt to conquer the Chinese market, they handed over a single email address. It cost a man ten years of his life.
viral_ideas.thumbnail_concept: A dark internet screen with red target brackets, bold text: 'BETRAYED'.

viral_ideas.title: The Golden Scam: The Man Who Sold a Fake Gold Mine
viral_ideas.topic: The Bre-X mining scandal in the Indonesian jungle.
viral_ideas.hook: A geologist, a businessman, and a jungle in Borneo. They claimed they found the largest gold deposit in human history. It was all a complete illusion, made with gold dust from a wedding ring.
viral_ideas.thumbnail_concept: A massive open pit mine with a golden vortex, bold text: 'FAKE GOLD'.

primary_playlist.title: Dark Business Empires
primary_playlist.description: Dark business history documentaries about monopolies, corporate scandals, cartels, and empires built on power.
primary_playlist.rule: Every Corporate Shadows long-form upload must be added to this playlist before or immediately after public release.

publishing_cadence.rule: Keep cadence even if a video is 95 percent ready, unless the blocker is legal, factual, severe brand risk, or missing final approval.

chapters.source: script scene titles and estimated scene durations
chapters.rule: Every description must include chapters starting at 0:00 before upload.

action_thresholds.ctr_watch: 5-8%: monitor for 72 hours and compare topic difficulty
action_thresholds.ctr_good: 8%+: keep angle and create variants from the same pattern
